details.text: Derivative: الْمُزَّمِّلُ in origin comes from مُتَزَمِّل (Mutazammil) where the Taa’ (تاء - ت) and the Zaa’ (زاء - ز) become fused to become a doubled Zaa’ with a Shaddah (ّ). It comes from the verb تَزَمَّلَ which means someone who is wrapped up in clothing or a garment, like a blanket or cloak. To be wrapped up (الْمُزَّمِّلُ) implies a comfortable situation in contrast to المُدَّثِّر (Al-Muddaththir) which means to be covered, which is used when someone wraps up due to the cold or fear.
Derivative: ذَرْنِي means leave me i.e., to deal with something or someone.
Derivative: النَّعْمَةِ means great luxury in life.
Derivative: أَنكَالًا means heavy fetters or chains to confine and restrict.
Derivative: غُصَّة describes food that is not easy to swallow, which you will choke on and doesn’t pass through the throat due to having thorns or other impediments.
Derivative: كَثِيبًا means to be turned into piles of conglomerated sand.
Derivative: وَبِيلًا means to be very stern, severe and tough.
Derivative: تَتَّقُونَ from اِتَّقَى – يَتَّقِي which means to protect yourself.
Derivative: مَفْعُولًا means “Done” i.e., it is a done deal and nothing can undo it.

- “And loan to Allah a beautiful loan”: A قَرْض (Qard) is a loan which you expect to get repaid. قَرْضًا حَسَنًا (Qard Hasan – Beautiful loan) is a loan that you do not expect to get back at a given time in order to help someone like an open ended loan and is almost like a Sadaqah. To give Allah a قَرْضًا حَسَنًا (a beautiful loan) means to spend your wealth or even time and health, all of which Allah gave to us, for the sake of Allah whilst leaving the terms of repayment to Allah. When a human asks for a loan from another it is from a position of weakness, however Allah, who is free of all need, uses the terminology of giving a loan to make it known to us that He will repay and reward us for what we spend for His sake.
Derivative: رَتِّلِ from رَتَّلَ – تَرْتِيلًا means to recite in a slow, clearly expressed making sure every letter is clearly pronounced and rhythmic manner.
Derivative: أَقْوَمُ means the most upright i.e., the best and most appropriate.

Derivative: وَطْئًا comes from وَطِئَ which means to step on the ground and leave an imprint. أَشَدُّ وَطْئًا means that it will leave the most intense or strongest imprint i.e., have the greatest impact or be the hardest hitting.

Derivative: سَبْحًا means relentless activity. It comes from سَبَحَ which means to swim. When someone is swimming, they can’t just stop and must keep in motion. From this it came to be used for relentless activity and being busy with many things.
Derivative: تَبَتَّلْ is the command form of تَبَتَّلَ – تَبَتُلًا and it means to cut yourself off and devote yourself to worshipping Allah.
Derivative: تَبْتِيلًا is from بَتَّلَ which means to cut off.

- When the Mafhoom Mutlaq (Here it is تَبْتِيلًا) is different in its form from the form of the verb that it is emphasising (Here it is تَبَتَّلْ), it represents a way in Arabic of combining both meanings into the phrase or statement i.e., here to cut oneself off and to cut off.

Derivative: وَكِيلًا is the One who rely on and entrust to take care of your problems and dispose of your affairs. وَكَالَة (Wakaalah) means to leave an issue in somebody else’s hands, to delegate it to him to dispose of or to handle for you. تَوَكُّل (Tawakkul) comes from it which means to be reliant upon Allah.
